title: Merging Maps in Clickhouse by keeping latest values
description:
### Company or project name
_No response_
### Question
GOAL IS AS FOLLOWS:
Incrementally update per-entity (Name) metadata stored as maps (metadata_s, metadata_n, metadata_b) in ClickHouse, allowing partial/sparse inserts (updating only some maps or keys at a time) while:
- Overwriting existing keys with the chronologically latest value.
- Preserving all unchanged keys from previous inserts.
- Never resetting unrelated maps or keys to empty due to partial updates.
In short: Achieve true "merge with latest overwrite" semantics for dynamic key-value metadata per entity, with efficient sparse updates.
Success Criteria (Brief)
- Partial insert of one map column leaves the other map columns completely unchanged.
- When a key is updated in a later insert, the new value replaces the old one.
- New keys are added without affecting existing ones.
- No client-side read-merge-write required (ideally native table/engine behavior).
- Final query (e.g., with FINAL or aggregation) shows the correct merged latest state for each entity.
**This is the close that i can get, but using mapMax keeps the maximum value, but i want to keep the latest value not the maximum value.**
https://fiddle.clickhouse.com/891172a1-9475-4ec5-a6e2-bf30ed689e02
**### FROM FIDDLE**
`CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS aggregated_attributes
(
Name String,
metadata_s SimpleAggregateFunction(maxMap, Map(String, String)),
metadata_n SimpleAggregateFunction(maxMap, Map(String, Float64)),
metadata_b SimpleAggregateFunction(maxMap, Map(String, UInt8))
)
ENGINE = AggregatingMergeTree
ORDER BY Name;
INSERT INTO aggregated_attributes (Name, metadata_s)
VALUES
('Foo', {'key1': 'value1', 'key2': 'value2'}),
('Bar', {'key1': 'value1', 'key2': 'value2'});
SELECT *
FROM aggregated_attributes FINAL;
INSERT INTO aggregated_attributes (Name, metadata_n)
VALUES
('Foo', {'key1': 5,'key2': 10});
SELECT *
FROM aggregated_attributes FINAL;
INSERT INTO aggregated_attributes (Name, metadata_s)
VALUES ('Foo', {'key1':'value1', 'key2':'valueNew2', 'key3': 'value3'}),('Bar', {'key1':'valueNew','key2':'value2'});
SELECT *
FROM aggregated_attributes FINAL;
INSERT INTO aggregated_attributes (Name, metadata_n)
VALUES
('Foo', {'key1': 10,'key2': 5, 'key3': 0}),
('Bar', {'key1': 5,'key2': 10});
SELECT *
FROM aggregated_attributes FINAL;`
**Results in clickhouse fiddle**
Name	metadata_s	metadata_n	metadata_b
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS aggregated_attributes (Name VARCHAR(255), metadata_s MAP(VARCHAR(255), VARCHAR(255)), metadata_n MAP(VARCHAR(255), DOUBLE PRECISION), metadata_b MAP(VARCHAR(255), BOOLEAN));
INSERT INTO aggregated_attributes (Name, metadata_s) VALUES ('Foo', MAP('key1', 'value1', 'key2', 'value2')), ('Bar', MAP('key1', 'value1', 'key2', 'value2'));
INSERT INTO aggregated_attributes (Name, metadata_n) VALUES ('Foo', MAP('key1', 5, 'key2', 10));
INSERT INTO aggregated_attributes (Name, metadata_s) VALUES ('Foo', MAP('key1', 'value1', 'key2', 'valueNew2', 'key3', 'value3')), ('Bar', MAP('key1', 'valueNew', 'key2', 'value2'));
SELECT Name, metadata_s, metadata_n FROM aggregated_attributes WHERE Name = 'Foo';
key_bug_features: Merging maps while preserving latest values during updates
